transcript.text: doing?
I'm well, thank you.
This meeting is being recorded.
I'm good.
Let me kick that out.
I apologize.
This thing just automatically always joins and I should be able to kick it out, hopefully.
I'm going to claim host.
Oh, anyway, I think it's okay.
Yeah, I don't mind.
It's just Fathom.
I am doing okay.
Congratulations on a new job.
I didn't realize that you had just switched.
Yeah, yeah.
Let me see.
It's funny because I found out about the company many months ago and then it was like, oh, this is the wave of the future.
I really like how they're doing this.
And then I was just talking to some of the folks and then maybe in November, early November, Tucker was like, hey, I don't know if you're expecting this, but let me know.
I love you.
You're interested.
And so, yeah, that's how it came up.
And so it was great because I don't know if you've attended any of Marcel's workshops where he's talking about like, hey, here's how to use AI for marketing.
Here's what I built before.
And funnily enough, like he and I had crossed paths before, but like back in when we were both at HP.
And so that was kind of the funny thing.
So, yeah.
How have you been?
I've been doing well.
We moved.
Well, since you left me at Sosta, I moved from California to Sun Valley, Idaho.
Oh, nice.
Yeah.
So doing a lot of skiing and we have three little kids now.
It's been a fantastic move and I still spend so much time in the Bay Area.
In fact, my address is still in the Bay Area, but we've been effectively raising kids out here.
Which has been awesome.
And yeah, I've taken that program manager, you know, when I was.
At Sosta, was supporting Ken and Tal in technical program management.
And from Sosta, went with the acquisition to Akamai.
From Akamai, went to New Relic.
And then from New Relic, made it to a smaller observability or telemetry pipeline startup called Mesmo.
Oh, okay.
And doing a lot of their business operations and supporting the executive team.
Right.
And all fine and well, but Mesmo's like kind of preparing for an acquisition.
So they cut down like 60% of their workforce or something like that, which as someone in business operations wasn't a shock.
I was fine.
But I've been applying for jobs since September.
And whoa, is it really crappy out there?
It's .
I think, yeah, I'm just hearing all about it.
And I've noticed that startups are the places where recruiters are still pinging.
which is like different.
Yeah.
I, um, I think I've been really good about being patient.
I'm looking for a remote job.
I'm looking for, uh, like a company size in between like 100 and 500.
Um, I'm looking for, like, I I'm being pretty specific.
And so, you know, I'm not casting a really wide net.
So I do need to be patient.
Um, I'm really looking for like product operations and business operations.
Um, and it's discouraging because it really does feel like a numbers game sometimes.
Like there can be five great candidates and any of the five would be a great fit for the role, but they still have to pick their favorite.
And it's so competitive right now.
Um, I mean, some of these roles I apply for like 3,500 other applicants.
It's wild.
Oh my God.
No, I think it's discouraging.
I don't think I would ever do that.
I think.
Now I only really respond to like recruiters paying me because like even with referrals, it's hard to like get like that first interview.
I was like, oh, this is this is ridiculous.
Yeah, it's it's been really trying.
I did come across GrowthX, though.
And one, I think the tool is really interesting, especially talking to somebody in marketing who would be.
But I don't know what your ICP is, but I'm guessing it's somebody in product marketing.
Yeah, I think it's actually you know, what's done really well for us is we have these fractional CMOs and one which like brought us to both our accounts.
But typically it's someone like I have one customer who's like a eight person startup and the CEO just does not have time to do this.
And then we have other companies which are huge and they already have like a ton of content, but they're not showing up.
And, like, ChatGPT and all the generative, like, chat bots.
And so I think those companies are, you're right, like, it's typically a marketing person who is our champion.
And it's usually someone who's, like, I don't get this new AI, GEO thing, so, like, help us out.

So, yeah, I think what's different about it is, like, we'll, for some larger, like, key customers with huge brands, we'll do, like, very custom stuff, like, for Lovable, we went and did, like, their, we created these templates so that if you didn't know what you wanted to create in Lovable, like, you could just go in and pick one of those.
And it's still content pretty much, but we're primarily, like, like, engines that, like, won't just work with SEO, but, like, specifically AI and GEO.
And what's neat about this role, I think, is we're not just building, like, these, we're not just building people to do this work, we're...
We're building the product behind it to do this.
So in a way, we're using, we're dogfooding the product as well.
Sure.
But we're also building kind of an AI visibility tool.
So there's a lot of like actual engineering stuff behind everything.
And it's pretty neat.
Like they actually got like investors and it's impressive how fast they've grown since I've been like watching them.
That's incredible.
What do you think of the executive team?
I really like Marcel.
really like, like everyone's very thoughtful.
They, unlike some of them, they weren't at each other's necks.
Like, sure, there's disagreements and stuff, but I feel like they were, like, they're all like very much aligned.
So at least I'm not hearing like huge, major like things.
Um, so it's, it's pretty good.
Like, uh, yeah.
And then I just read.
It's funny, the recruiter had just pinged me because one of the things I was worried about was I looked at the last door reviews and I'm like, oh my God, this is crap.
This is like a  show.
And then I, but I knew like the people who had talked to me and I knew Marcel, so I'm like, ah, this is, they probably like do a lot of like hires.
And then because the role is so specific, if you don't make it, they're like, okay, well, we can't like, we can't work.
This won't work out.
So I think they do a lot of that like quick, um, like they go through people quickly that way, but I feel like, uh, they're a little bit more intentional about it now.
Yeah.

I mean, glass door is really tricky in my opinion, because really angry people go on glass door, like a collection of glass door reviews are not necessarily a great represented, you know, representation of what it is to work there.
Um, they're definitely, like, like,

Sometimes you read Glassdoor reviews and it's all about pivoting strategy and unaligned leadership and stuff like that that I think is a little bit more concerning than just disgruntled employees who didn't fit.
You know what I mean?
I haven't read GrowthX's Glassdoor, but I know that Mesmo, where I just was, had a lot of  reviews.
I mean, some of them were really based in truth.
Like, you know, we did pivot our strategy just too frequently that things weren't getting done.
Projects weren't completed.
A lot of engineers felt like they were wasting their time.

And then other times some, you know, some people just don't have that startup mindset where if they see a problem, they fix it, even if it's outside of their realm of expertise or what their JD actually says.
Or, you know, that high threshold for pain in a way, like getting through those problems and finding that product market fit.
Some of the people at Mesmo just didn't cut it that way.
Is Lauren still there?
Yeah, Lauren's still there.
Oh my gosh.
Okay.
Yeah, that says a lot.
Yeah.
I'm uncomfortable being jobless.
It sucks, but I'm not sad to be moving on.
You know what I mean?
There's a difference for me.
I certainly don't miss what I was doing, and I'm very much excited for a new opportunity.
I just want to make sure that I'm applying to places that want me.
Right, right.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Not to mention, I was just reviewing GrowthX before we got on the call.
Their process is robust.
If I apply for this job, I have to turn in two videos.
Oh, really?
I didn't have to do that when I was there.
Okay.
One video about a project that I've completed that I'm proud of, and it's like a big deal.
Video, teaching something, any type of topic, just to, my guess is just to see your presentation skills.
Right, right, right.
Totally, especially with the volume of applicants lately, but, oh my God, these job applications are getting more and more complicated.
Right, it's pretty intense.
Although, I think for my panel, I definitely used a lot of AI to help, because I was like, there's no way I'm going to, but then, yeah, I think they ask a lot of questions.
But everyone I've, like, met and worked with, I've been pretty impressed with.
Like, I haven't, like, found one person where I'm like, ah, does it make sense?
Yeah, that's awesome.
Well, I have a favor to ask of you.
I, I don't know if you're still connected.
Oops, sorry.
Jeez.
When something rings, everything rings.
Um.
Um.
I don't know if you're still working with the recruiter at all, but I'd be really curious to hear if there's anybody far along in the process or if it's worth submitting an application.
I just asked, actually, they said they have one person in the final stage, but he's not sure if it will work out.
So I think it's what I can do is share your profile with him and then see where that goes next.
That would be amazing because if I'm going to record some videos this weekend, I'd like to do it for a good reason.
Right, right, right.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Kathy, I'd really appreciate that.
Any type of Intel or foot in the door is just making such a difference in this experience.
So I really hope someday I can pay it forward.
I really appreciate it.
Yeah, I'll do that.
Are there any other questions or anything?
I didn't look at company size.
Oh, they're like maybe a hundred percent.
And what series are they on?
think they just got Series A.
Let me check.
And then they got another round of SAFE funding.
Oh, okay.
So I think they raised a 12 million Series A.
And I think they were talking to like a couple of people, A16 or something like that.
So they're at Series A, yeah.
And let's see, company size.
Do you know anything about 2026 goals?
Is it like revenue or?
I think at least, I'm hoping it's at least 100% like jump.
And so I think in some of the webinars, I think they've talked a little bit about like, so, so originally they had like real.
High expectations for like end of year numbers, but I'm not sure, but like some of the things change and they didn't quite hit it.
So I don't know what the new revenue targets are right now, but yeah, I'm sure we're going to be doing some like, we'll probably be doing some planning and stuff around it.
All I know is my goal is to like, so I work in like the strategy sprints team, which is kind of like a paid PLC.
And so our goal is to basically renew our customers at the year long rate at like 90%.
So right now I have two customers, so we'll keep my fingers crossed.
That's an exciting time though.
Yeah, it is.
think I've worked for series A since SOSTA really.
Oh, yeah, yeah, because new relic in all of them is like, yeah, yeah, like, yeah.
Ackermire was public.
We Relic was public, I believe.
And Mesmo was like series C or D.
Okay.
Which, what is it?
Which size did you see?
I'm like, oh, this is a good fit for me.
I like companies that have rigorous goals, whether that be scaling or trying to meet some ARR goals.

I feel like business ops is like a really meaningful position at companies that are looking to hold themselves accountable, maybe build some new process, make sure that the company is aligned towards the same goal.
People understand why they're working hard on certain projects.
Yes.

Like, so any type of those opportunities for growth, whether that's, like I said, you know, you want to double your company size in 18 months or just have some like lofty ARR goals or whatever, whatever have you.
That's where I think.
Business ops gets really interesting.
I think that business or operations in general over companies of 500 tend to just be executing other people's process.
Yeah, that's not fun.
No, not fun.
So I do really like to support a leadership team, help them identify and build like really meaningful OKRs and get them over the finish line.
And that to me tends to be at that like 100 to 500 person company.
Yeah.
What else can I tell you?
Yeah, I also spent some time doing product operations, which works a lot with marketing.
Cool.
I think, yeah, I think I'm not sure if Daniel is looking for that as well.
But I think this will be helpful because one of their major focus points is on getting our internal product like customer facing.
Because right now, like the only people using it.
It are the internal folks using it to actually produce the content, but at some point, what we want to be able to do is make it customer-ready.
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
So I think that Marcel plays a role where not only is he probably our best sales guy, but he also works a lot with the engineering team to build up the product.
So it's pretty cool.
Yeah, I find that business operations can be a great conduit there.
Making sure that engineering is completing projects that the leadership team is looking for.
Or making sure the visions are aligned and making sure that timelines are communicated and there are no surprises and nothing goes from green to red, right?
Right, right, right.
Exactly.
Yeah.
We're doing quite a bit of that.
Like, internally, as we're looking at our clients, we're definitely looking at, like, hey, what's working?
And then what's...
Getting orange.
we need to like turn those around.
So yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Cool.
Well, I really appreciate it.
And I hope you have a wonderful weekend and a wonderful Friday.
I think my LinkedIn profile is pretty accurate.
I'm just giving it a look.
Or accurate is the wrong word.
I just want to make sure that there is a lot of good information on there.
Yeah.
I think that's, that's perfect.
If you send over my profile and see what happens.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Let's, let's do that.
But cool.
Thanks for, thanks for reaching out.
Wasn't expecting it.
And then we should catch up later sometime.
Yeah.
I'd love to pick your brain throughout this process.
Cool.
All right.
All right.
Take care.
Have a great weekend.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Stephanie explores a role at GrowthX via an internal referral from Kathy.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Stephanie's Search:** Seeking a remote Biz/Product Ops role at a 100–500 person company, a size where Ops can build process and drive strategy, not just execute.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Fit:** A Series A company (\~100 people) with a key goal to productize its internal AI content engine, creating a perfect strategic fit for Stephanie's Ops experience.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
  - [**Referral Action:** Kathy will refer Stephanie to the recruiter. A candidate is in final stages, but the outcome is uncertain, creating an opening.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)

## Topics

### Stephanie's Job Search

  - [**Context:** Laid off from Mesmo (a Series C/D startup) in September due to a 60% workforce reduction ahead of an acquisition.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=145.0)
  - [**Market Reality:** The job market is extremely competitive, with some roles attracting 3,500+ applicants.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=209.0)
  - [**Search Criteria:**](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
      - [**Role:** Business Operations or Product Operations](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
      - [**Size:** 100–500 employees](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
      - [**Location:** Remote](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**Rationale for Size:** This range offers the opportunity to build meaningful processes and drive strategy, avoiding the execution-only work common at larger companies.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=860.0)

### GrowthX Overview & Strategic Need

  - [**Product:** An AI/GEO content engine for marketing teams, used internally to create client content and being productized for direct customer use.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
  - [**Company Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
      - [**Size:** \~100 employees](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
      - [**Funding:** $12M Series A + SAFE round](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
      - [**Leadership:** Marcel (CEO) is highly regarded and deeply involved in product development.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)
  - [**Strategic Need:** Productize the internal AI engine to make it customer-facing.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
      - [**Significance:** This creates a strategic need for Biz/Product Ops to align leadership vision with engineering execution, a core strength of Stephanie's.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=986.0)

### Referral & Hiring Process

  - [**Hiring Process:** The application requires two videos: one on a past project and one teaching a topic (to assess presentation skills).](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [**Referral Status:** A candidate is in final stages, but the outcome is uncertain.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [**Action:** Kathy will refer Stephanie's LinkedIn profile to the recruiter to assess fit before Stephanie invests time in the video application.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Kathy:** Send Stephanie's LinkedIn profile to the GrowthX recruiter.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)
  - [**Stephanie:** Await feedback from the recruiter before creating the video application.](https://fathom.video/share/wEmegB8zABzzubJb66o5-xhZ7f-qgZNu?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)

action_items.description: Share Stephanie's LinkedIn w/ GrowthX recruiter re: Business/Product Ops role
